title: 3. What a Strange Country | Do We Get to Win This Time?
format: Brian Raftery's Vietnam-film history reaches 1978, when The Deer Hunter and Coming Home dragged the war onto the big screen and split the Oscars between them.

segments.heading: How Hollywood brought Vietnam to the screen
segments.note: 1977-79, told through the films that fought over the war

films.title: The Deer Hunter
films.note: Michael Cimino's three-hour epic, built entirely to reach its Russian roulette scene. De Niro, Walken, Savage, Cazale and Streep; nearly four hours in Cimino's cut before Verna Fields was secretly hired to trim it. Won Best Picture and Best Director at the 51st Oscars.

films.title: Coming Home
films.note: Jane Fonda's five-year passion project, from a love triangle between a paralyzed vet (Jon Voight) and her angry husband (Bruce Dern). Directed by Hal Ashby. Won acting Oscars for Fonda and Voight plus original screenplay.

films.title: The Green Berets
films.note: John Wayne's pro-war film, the old model the '78 films were reacting against; the military lent Wayne equipment it later denied Coming Home.

films.title: Twilight's Last Gleaming
films.note: Robert Aldrich's missile-silo thriller with Burt Lancaster as a POW blackmailing the president over Vietnam. Its ads never said the word 'Vietnam.'

films.title: Heroes
films.note: Henry Winkler as a struggling vet, sold as a Fonz-and-Sally-Field rom-com to hide the war.

films.title: Big Wednesday
films.note: John Milius's surf coming-of-age epic (Busey, Katt, Jan-Michael Vincent) with Vietnam in the background, marketed as a feel-good beach movie.

films.title: Soldier Blue
films.note: Referenced from the previous episode as a cavalry-vs-Native-Americans western read as a Vietnam allegory.

films.title: Ulzana's Raid
films.note: Aldrich and Lancaster's harsh cavalry western, another Vietnam allegory.

films.title: F.T.A.
films.note: Fonda and Donald Sutherland's anti-war revue documentary; pulled from theaters after her North Vietnam trip.

films.title: Midnight Express
films.note: Won adapted screenplay for a young veteran named Oliver Stone, whose own Vietnam scripts were still stalled.

films.title: Born on the Fourth of July
films.note: One of Stone's two Vietnam projects stuck in development limbo in 1979.

films.title: Platoon
films.note: Stone's other stalled Vietnam script, then titled 'The Platoon.'

films.title: Apocalypse Now
films.note: Teased at the close: Coppola, on the Oscar stage, still trying to finish the decade's maddest Vietnam film.

TV: The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson

Born on the Fourth of July.title: Born on the Fourth of July
